docs.title: Microsoft Build 2026 — Next Steps
docs.text: Microsoft Build is anchored in technical depth, developer credibility, and interactive creation. Engage with session-specific content in your environment with interactive repos to further your learning for your own purposes. Click on a topic to see the repos for each session.

docs.title: Session Topics
docs.text:
Explore lab and session repositories organized by topic to engage in interactive activities to further your learning from Microsoft Build. Click on each topic area to see the list of sessions and their associated repos to start.

Topic Description Agents & Apps Focuses on building applications and agents from a single prompt to agent framework for developers who are building for task to building for scale and doing both with security in mind. Cloud Platform & Data Focuses on the cloud foundations developers build on — from infrastructure, to data, to platform services — that power modern applications and AI workloads. Developer Tools & Frameworks Focuses on using coding agents, personalizing developer workflows, reviewing and securing the new volume of code, and integrating LLM inference across a variety of open source and proprietary frameworks like Next.js, Python, .NET, Java, and more. Model Training For developers and data scientists looking to make AI work for them focusing on fine-tuning, reinforcement training, evals, PyTorch, TensorFlow, and more. Responsible AI Reinforces Microsoft's leadership in helping developers ship AI that is safe, compliant, and resilient by design. Learn to build trustworthy, secure, and responsible AI systems. Windows Focuses on how developers can build on and build with Windows both natively and through WSL.

docs.title: Agents & Apps
docs.text: Explore the labs and session repositories from Microsoft Build 2026 focused on AI agents, copilots, and intelligent applications.

docs.title: Microsoft Agents Hub
docs.text: • Agents Hub — Central resource for discovering and building agents with Microsoft technologies

docs.title: Microsoft Foundry
docs.text:
• What is Microsoft Foundry Agent Service? — Overview and key capabilities of Foundry for building, testing, and deploying agents
  • Agent development lifecycle — End-to-end lifecycle from creation through production monitoring
  • Build collaborative, multi-agent systems with Connected Agents — Example patterns and implementation for multi-agent systems
  • Agent identity concepts in Microsoft Foundry — Identity and security management for agents

docs.title: Copilot Studio
docs.text:
• Copilot Studio overview — Low-code platform for building custom AI agents
  • Copilot Studio and agent samples — Collection of samples and learning resources for Copilot Studio
  • Choose between Microsoft 365 Copilot and Copilot Studio to build your agent — Guidance on selecting the right platform for your needs

docs.title: Multi-Agent Systems & Architecture
docs.text:
• Single agent or multiple agents — Decision framework for single vs. multi-agent system design
  • Process to build agents across your organization — Organizational approach to agent orchestration and secure implementation
  • Agent system design patterns — Design patterns and architectural approaches for agent systems

docs.title: Learning & Development
docs.text:
• Orchestrate a multi-agent solution using Semantic Kernel — Training module on building multi-agent solutions with Semantic Kernel SDK
  • Adopt, extend and build Copilot experiences across the Microsoft Cloud — Comprehensive guide to building Copilot solutions

docs.title: Cloud-Native Platform (AKS)
docs.text:
• What is Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS)? - Core AKS concepts and architecture for production workloads.
  • MLOps best practices in AKS - Guidance for deploying, operating, and scaling AI/ML workloads on AKS.
  • AKS performance and scaling best practices - Node, image, and scaling recommendations for reliable cluster performance.
  • Azure Linux Container Host for AKS - Overview of Azure Linux for container-focused AKS node pools.

docs.title: Data Platforms and Analytics (Microsoft Fabric)
docs.text:
• Microsoft Fabric overview - End-to-end analytics and data platform capabilities.
  • End-to-end data lifecycle in Fabric - Ingest, transform, analyze, and operationalize data across OneLake and Fabric workloads.
  • Analyze and train data in Fabric - Data science, MLflow, and AI-enabled analytics workflows in Fabric.

docs.title: Databases for AI Apps and Agents
docs.text:
• Intelligent applications and AI in Azure SQL Database - Patterns for RAG, vector search, and AI-integrated application design.
  • AI agents in Azure Database for PostgreSQL - Build agent workflows with PostgreSQL vector capabilities and Foundry Agent Service.
  • Semantic search with Azure Database for PostgreSQL and Azure OpenAI - Hands-on tutorial for semantic retrieval over Postgres data.
  • AI agents in Azure Cosmos DB - Design guidance for agent memory, vector search, and globally distributed NoSQL workloads.
  • Azure Cosmos DB integrations for AI applications - Semantic Kernel, LangChain, LlamaIndex, and related integration references.

docs.title: Reliability and Resiliency
docs.text:
• Azure reliability overview - Reliability concepts, capabilities, and service guidance across Azure.
  • AKS architecture best practices (Well-Architected) - Reliability, security, and operational excellence guidance for AKS.
  • Reliability testing strategy - Fault injection and chaos engineering recommendations for resilient cloud systems.
